title: Legousia biflora
taxonomy: Legousia biflora is a member of the family Campanulaceae. It belongs to the genus Legousia, which is a group of flowering plants. Legousia biflora is a species of plant that is native to parts of Europe. The plant is also known as two-flowered Legousia or two-flowered legousia. It is a small, delicate perennial with blue flowers.
uses: Legousia biflora, also known as the alpine legousia or alpine blue-eyed grass, has been used in traditional medicine in various parts of the world. Its roots and leaves are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential treatment for various health issues. The plant has been used to treat digestive problems, such as diarrhea and indigestion, as well as to reduce fever and relieve pain. However, further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Legousia biflora for medicinal purposes.

intro: Legousia biflora, also known as the common forget-me-not or hairy bellflower, is a perennial plant that belongs to the borage family. Traditionally, this plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
